description: F&B brands celebrate Western holidays (Halloween, Christmas, etc.) with themed team costumes and festive workplace atmosphere, positioning the brand as culturally inclusive and fun-loving.
structural_recipe: Post behind-the-scenes team photos in holiday costumes/decorations. Use holiday greetings in English/Spanish mixed with Spanish exclamations. Emphasize team participation and employee enjoyment. Tag the holiday and use fire/celebration emojis. Include casual invitation language to customers.
why_it_works: Young, urban Saudi audiences, particularly in cosmopolitan areas, appreciate international brand authenticity and inclusive workplace culture. Celebrating Western holidays signals modernity, globalism, and a fun company culture that attracts younger demographics seeking Western-influenced experiences.
transplantation_caveats: Halloween and some Western holidays have historically faced sensitivity in conservative Saudi circles; monitor audience sentiment
May alienate traditional or religiously conservative customer segments
Risk of backlash if perceived as promoting non-Islamic values; frame as employee appreciation rather than religious celebration
Language mixing (English/Spanish) may reduce accessibility for Arabic-primary audiences
cultural_constraints: Avoid presenting Western holidays as superior to Islamic holidays
Ensure no religious mockery or disrespect in costume choices
Balance with local/Islamic holiday celebrations to maintain cultural neutrality
